title: 5. Attack of the Clones | Gene and Roger
format: Part five of Brian Raftery's Gene and Roger series: the 80s wave of Siskel-and-Ebert imitators, and why none of the copycats could match the original pair.

segments.heading: Attack of the clones
segments.note: The critics who tried to replace Siskel and Ebert

films.title: Platoon
films.note: The clip both replacement duos leaned on: Medved and Lyons praising it on the new Sneak Previews, Reed and Harris arguing over it on the relaunched At the Movies.

films.title: Full Metal Jacket
films.note: Rex Reed and Bill Harris review it on At the Movies, sliding into a Platoon comparison.

films.title: Project X
films.note: The Matthew Broderick movie about rescuing a lab chimp, reviewed by the teens on Nickelodeon's Rated K, who debated it against Siskel and Ebert.

films.title: King Kong
films.note: Leonard Maltin, reviewing for Entertainment Tonight, plugged Criterion's Laserdisc restoration of it in 1985.

films.title: Red Heat
films.note: Cited as an example of the era's throwaway blurb raves: 'Red Heat is red hot.'

films.title: Some Kind of Wonderful
films.note: Another blurb-bait title from the same run of 80s billboard raves.

TV: Rated K: For Kids by Kids
